macro_segments.title: Introduction to Rabbi Eliezer's Position on Machshirei Milah and the Mavoi Digression

micro_segments.title: Opening: Rabbi Eliezer's position that preparatory acts of bris milah override Shabbat
The case of carrying a knife through courtyards and the question of eruvei chatzrot
Introduction to the complex discussion of mavoi and shituf chatzrot

macro_segments.title: The Paradox of Eruvei Chatzrot and the Status of Mavoi

micro_segments.title: Itmar: the teaching of Rabbi Zeira regarding mavoi without shituf
Abaye's clarification: Rabbah bar Avuha's explanation of Rabbi Zeira's statement
The ironic principle: when courtyards make eruv together with houses, the mavoi becomes forbidden
Rav Chanina Chuza'ah's explanation: courtyards transformed into houses by eruv
Rav's position: mavoi requires lachi and korah and classic configuration of houses and courtyards

macro_segments.title: Bitul Reshut and the Gemara's Rejection of Explanations

micro_segments.title: The Gemara's counterargument: bitul reshut allows houses to waive their domain
The problem of sof sof: even with bitul reshut, you don't have classic configuration
Proposed solution: dividing the day—first half for one person, second half for another
Rav Ashi's simpler explanation: what makes courtyards forbidden is the presence of houses

macro_segments.title: Return to Rabbi Eliezer: Scope of His Position on Machshirei Mitzvot

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Chiya bar Abba in the name of Rabbi Yochanan: Rabbi Eliezer's ruling is not universal
The example of shtei halechem: learned via gezeirah shavah, not general principle
Baraitot: Rabbi Eliezer extends his position to lulav, sukkah, matzah, and shofar

macro_segments.title: Excluding Tzitzit and Mezuzah from Rabbi Eliezer's Rule

micro_segments.title: Rav Adda bar Ahava: tzitzit and mezuzah are excluded from Rabbi Eliezer's position
Consensus: even Rabbi Eliezer agrees one is liable for putting tzitzit or mezuzah on Shabbat
Rav Yosef's reasoning: they have no fixed time, so they don't override Shabbat
Abaye's counterargument: every day is a fixed obligation, so they should override Shabbat
Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak's explanation: these obligations are contingent on ownership

macro_segments.title: Lulav: Learning from the Pasuk that it Overrides Shabbat

micro_segments.title: The question: where does Rabbi Eliezer learn that lulav and its preparations override Shabbat
The pasuk: ulekachtem lachem bayom harishon—the word bayom teaches override even on Shabbat
Why the word bayom must teach machshirin, not just the basic mitzvah
The rabbis' position: the word bayom teaches daytime, not nighttime
Why lulav cannot be the source for other mitzvot: it has four components (arba minim)

macro_segments.title: Sukkah: Learning via Gezeirah Shavah from Lulav

micro_segments.title: The question: where does Rabbi Eliezer learn that sukkah and its preparations override Shabbat
Gezeirah shavah on 'shivas yamim': both lulav and sukkah are associated with seven days
Why sukkah cannot be the source: it applies at night and day, unlike most other mitzvot

macro_segments.title: Matzah: Learning via Gezeirah Shavah from Sukkah

micro_segments.title: The question: where does Rabbi Eliezer learn that matzah and its preparations override Shabbat
Gezeirah shavah on 'chamisha asar': both sukkot and matzah are associated with the fifteenth
Why matzah cannot be the source: it applies equally to men and women, unlike other mitzvot

macro_segments.title: Shofar: Learning from the Pasuk Yom Teruah Yiheyeh Lachem

micro_segments.title: The question: where does Rabbi Eliezer learn that shofar and its preparations override Shabbat
The pasuk: Yom teruah yiheyeh lachem—the word bayom teaches override even on Shabbat
Why the word bayom must teach machshirin: blowing shofar is not a melacha
The rabbis' position: bayom teaches daytime only, learned from shofar of Yom Kippur
Why shofar cannot be the source: it has special power in bringing Israel's remembrance to God

macro_segments.title: Milah: Learning from the Pasuk U'Vayom Hashmini Yimmol Basar Orlato

micro_segments.title: The question: where does Rabbi Eliezer learn that milah and its preparations override Shabbat
Why milah cannot be learned from other mitzvot: milah can be delayed to another day unlike others
The pasuk: u'vayom hashmini yimmol—the word bayom teaches override even on Shabbat
The extra word bayom tells us machshirin override Shabbat, not just the milah itself
Why milah cannot be the source: it has thirteen covenants mentioned in the Torah, giving it special weight

topical_tags.term: Eruv / Eruvei Chatzrot (joining for Shabbat carrying)
Lachi and Korah (beams used to permit carrying in mavoi)
Rabbi Eliezer vs. Chachamim (main dispute on machshirei mitzvot)
Shtei Halechem (two loaves of bread on Shavuot)
Gezeirah Shavah (verbal analogy in biblical exegesis)
Lulav and Arba Minim (four species on Sukkot)
Machshirin Dochen et Shabbat (preparatory acts override Shabbat)
Bayom (on the day - word indicating override)
Rav vs. Rabbanan (dispute on mavoi structure)
Rav Chanina Chuza'ah (explaining the mavoi paradox)
Rabbah bar Avuha (interpreter of Rabbi Zeira)
Rabbi Yochanan (source of Eliezer's non-universal rule)
Rav Adda bar Ahava (excluding tzitzit and mezuzah)
Halacha le-Moshe mi-Sinai (unwritten law from Sinai)
Yom Kippur Shofar (special case of shofar)
